textDisplay: Being Indian is such a shame. We want this movie to top the charts of box office but we don't want to support our own Film industry (particularly south Indian film industry) which has so much potential. We wait for Indian movies to come to ott but we go to theatres to watch these American movies. The money these movies earn from us ultimately makes America stronger and eventually our GDP becomes weaker. We call ourselves more patriotic than any other country but reality is we don't do anything for our country's development. I'm ashamed of being called Indian.
textOriginal: Being Indian is such a shame. We want this movie to top the charts of box office but we don't want to support our own Film industry (particularly south Indian film industry) which has so much potential. We wait for Indian movies to come to ott but we go to theatres to watch these American movies. The money these movies earn from us ultimately makes America stronger and eventually our GDP becomes weaker. We call ourselves more patriotic than any other country but reality is we don't do anything for our country's development. I'm ashamed of being called Indian.

textDisplay:
Not a fan of this trailer at all. Most of the cool shots/bits were already in the much, much more appealing teaser, the background music is completely unnoticeable, it lacks energy (those two are often linked)... very disappointing. I have no doubt the movie will be sensational, but that's disappointing.
Three important female characters, though... it's getting crowded. When Vanessa Kirby appeared in Fallout, I was like okay, it's not just Rebecca Ferguson then...? Then Fallout dealt with it very well... but this time, again, three, with the Marvel chick, who's much, much less charismatic than Ferguson and Kirby... we'll see.
textOriginal:
Not a fan of this trailer at all. Most of the cool shots/bits were already in the much, much more appealing teaser, the background music is completely unnoticeable, it lacks energy (those two are often linked)... very disappointing. I have no doubt the movie will be sensational, but that's disappointing.
Three important female characters, though... it's getting crowded. When Vanessa Kirby appeared in Fallout, I was like okay, it's not just Rebecca Ferguson then...? Then Fallout dealt with it very well... but this time, again, three, with the Marvel chick, who's much, much less charismatic than Ferguson and Kirby... we'll see.

textDisplay:
This is decent, but where are the new generation of movie stars?  It looks like Tom Cruise is the last one left.  Not being funny, but I don't want to see a 60 year old man as an international spy with a couple of sidekick women who are not hot in the slightest.

Instead the studios would rather drop another $300m on a guaranteed seat filler that no one will ever watch after 2023 instead of taking a chance and coming up with something original. The Disney execs are the same.
textOriginal:
This is decent, but where are the new generation of movie stars?  It looks like Tom Cruise is the last one left.  Not being funny, but I don't want to see a 60 year old man as an international spy with a couple of sidekick women who are not hot in the slightest.

Instead the studios would rather drop another $300m on a guaranteed seat filler that no one will ever watch after 2023 instead of taking a chance and coming up with something original. The Disney execs are the same.
